Setting the Server Timezone on Heroku
Smaller projects sometimes need to display local time-of-day, but Heroku's default server time isn't right out of the box. Quick note on setting it.
I've used Heroku for a lot of project servers, but some projects need correct local time. Without setup, Heroku runs at
, so anything that formats time with local defaults shows UTC:
The fix is simple — set a Heroku environment variable that tells the dyno which timezone to use.
Open the Heroku Settings page → find
. That's where you set Heroku environment variables (
and Value to your tz-database timezone (e.g.,
Save and restart the dyno — the time will now reflect your chosen zone:
language-cmd shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast
For the full list of valid timezone names, check Wikipedia's
List of tz database time zones

Heroku defaults to UTC for server time, which is fine for logs but wrong for any app that displays local-time-of-day to users. Fix it by setting the TZ environment variable to a tz database name (like Asia/Taipei or America/New_York). This post covers both routes — Heroku dashboard and the CLI.
